comment_text.snp4.3:1.1: Duṭṭhamana, or more commonly paduṭṭhamana, means “malice” not “corruption”, as is found through the suttas and confirmed by the Niddesa.
comment_text.snp4.3:2.2: Bodhi, Norman, and Ñāṇadīpa all render chanda as “desire”, and that is certainly the sense of chandānunīto at SN 35.94:6.4. Here, however, Niddesa glosses with synonyms for “view” and this seems like a more plausible sense. Note too the sense of niviṭṭha as “dogmatic”, which recurs through the Aṭṭhakavagga (cp. abhinivesa).
comment_text.snp4.3:2.3: Samattāni is regularly used of vows “undertaken”, and given the following verse this is surely the meaning here, contra Niddesa and commentary. See Snp 4.4:5.1, where samādāya is used in the same sense.
comment_text.snp4.3:2.4: Here we find a sense of jānāti that doesn’t fit the English idea of “knowledge” as “true belief”. The idea is that they speak from their own (limited and opinionated) ideas. It’s not as pejorative as “opinion” nor as solid as “knowledge”, so I render as “notion”. We find this usage commonly in the final two chapters of Snp.
comment_text.snp4.3:4.4: Ussada, here rendered “pretension”, is a poetic term with the literal sense of “swelling”. In this chapter it has a close connection with “pride” (Snp 4.10:8.4, Snp 4.14:6.4; cf. the English sense of pride as “protuberance”), although this connection is less obvious elsewhere (Snp 3.6:14.4, Iti 97:8.4, and Ud 1.4:2.1 = Kd 1:2.3.6). Its absence is a sign of a true brahmin (MN 98:11.19, Dhp 400:2, Snp 3.9:36.1). The Niddesa does not link it with pride especially, rather explaining it as sevenfold: greed, hate, delusion, conceit, views, corruptions, and deeds (Mnd 10:105.4).
comment_text.snp4.3:8.3: The problem addressed is one who is picking up one thing after another. It’s therefore best to translate atta in an active sense, per Niddesa: gahaṇaṁ muñcanā samatikkanto. We find similar phrases describing the living arahant in such prose passages as SN 22.79:14.1.

root_text.snp4.3:8.3: Attā nirattā na hi tassa atthi,

translation_text.snp4.3:1.2: while others speak set on truth.
translation_text.snp4.3:1.3: When disputes come up a sage does not get involved,
translation_text.snp4.3:1.4: which is why they’ve no barrenness at all.
translation_text.snp4.3:2.1: How can you transcend your own view
translation_text.snp4.3:2.2: when you’re led by preference, dogmatic in belief?
translation_text.snp4.3:2.4: you’d speak according to your notion.
translation_text.snp4.3:3.2: of their own precepts and vows.
translation_text.snp4.3:3.3: They have an ignoble nature, <j>say those who are skilled,
translation_text.snp4.3:3.4: since they speak on themselves of their own accord.
translation_text.snp4.3:4.2: never boasts “thus am I” of their precepts.
translation_text.snp4.3:4.3: They have a noble nature, say those who are skilled,
translation_text.snp4.3:4.4: who have no pretensions about anything in the world.
translation_text.snp4.3:5.1: For one who formulates and creates teachings,
translation_text.snp4.3:5.2: and promotes them despite their defects,
translation_text.snp4.3:5.3: if they see an advantage for themselves,
translation_text.snp4.3:5.4: they become dependent on that, <j>relying on unstable peace.
translation_text.snp4.3:6.1: It’s not easy to get over dogmatic views
translation_text.snp4.3:6.2: adopted after judging among the teachings.
translation_text.snp4.3:6.3: That’s why, among all these dogmas, a person
translation_text.snp4.3:6.4: ditches one teaching and takes up another.
translation_text.snp4.3:7.1: The cleansed one has no formulated view
translation_text.snp4.3:7.2: at all in the world about the different realms.
translation_text.snp4.3:7.3: Having given up illusion and conceit,
translation_text.snp4.3:7.4: by what path would they go? They are not involved.
translation_text.snp4.3:8.1: For one who gets involved is embroiled in disputes about teachings—
translation_text.snp4.3:8.2: but how to dispute with the uninvolved? <j>About what?
translation_text.snp4.3:8.3: For picking up and putting down <j>is not what they do;
translation_text.snp4.3:8.4: they have shaken off all views in this very life.

variant_text.snp4.3:3.2: paresa → parassa (si, mr) | pāva → pāvā (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)

reference_text.snp4.3:5.1: bj25.248, ms18Sn_954, pts-vp-pli154, sya25.488, vnp784, vns791
